evidence: Flagged duplication between ExplorationHandlers.java:4 and CollocateProfileComparator.java:8 is import block boilerplate — this is not design duplication; both files have distinct responsibilities and no meaningful structural overlap in logic.

SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries uses a callback (BiFunction) to share loop logic between surface and dependency sketches — effective extraction of a repeated structural pattern into a data-driven loop.

ExplorationHandlers.buildExploreRequest is a shared preamble that collapses three repeated parameter-extraction steps (seeds, relation, params) for multi-seed handlers — good extraction; reduces 3-way repetition.

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldComparison has a slightly different parameter surface (no 'relation' param, different preconditions) compared to handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, leading to two similar but not identical handler bodies — not deep nesting, but structural similarity without full extraction.
CorpusQueryHandlers.parseBcqlRequest is cleanly extracted from handleCorpusQuery — appropriate single-responsibility split.
SemanticFieldExplorer acts as a thin facade over MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator — each class has a focused single responsibility, no god-class patterns.
GrammarConfigLoader has two private parse phases (parseAndValidateVersion, validateNoLegacyKeys, parseRelations, parseRelation) each doing one thing — well-decomposed.

BlackLabQueryExecutor.collectFrequenciesFromGroups takes a Function<String, String> lemmaExtractor parameter to share iteration logic across three different grouping strategies — data-driven design that avoids copy-paste.

strengths: SketchHandlers.executeRelationQueries callback pattern eliminates repetitive surface/dep sketch loops and makes the variation point explicit.
SemanticFieldExplorer is a proper facade with no logic of its own, delegating to focused collaborators.
GrammarConfigLoader is cleanly decomposed into small, single-purpose private methods with no over-long methods.
BlackLabQueryExecutor.collectFrequenciesFromGroups centralises grouping iteration with an injected extractor function, avoiding three near-identical loops.
ExploreResponseAssembler is a pure data-transformation class with no HTTP or business logic — clean single responsibility.
design_coherence.issue_character: The boilerplate duplication flagged by the mechanical detector is limited to import blocks and is a false positive; the genuine design concern is that ExplorationHandlers contains three similar explore-response-building methods that share structure but are not fully unified due to parameter set differences.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase shows mature design instincts: callback-based loops, facade delegation, and single-method classes all appear in the right places. The score is held in the mid-70s because the exploration handlers have a family of three similar handler methods (explore, explore-multi, compare) that partially share structure via buildExploreRequest and buildBaseExploreResponse but the comparison handler does not participate in the shared preamble — this asymmetry creates a maintenance surface where a parameter change to the shared params must be applied in some but not all handlers.

issues.summary: Comparison handler does not use the shared buildExploreRequest preamble, creating inconsistent parameter extraction paths
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti both call resolveRelationConfig and resolveExplorationParams via buildExploreRequest (or inline equivalent).

handleSemanticFieldComparison independently calls HttpApiUtils.requireParam for 'seeds', then resolveExplorationParams — it does not call resolveRelationConfig (intentionally, since comparison aggregates across all relations), creating a structural asymmetry in how parameters are extracted across the three handlers.
If resolveExplorationParams gains a new parameter, the comparison handler must be updated separately from the shared preamble.
issues.suggestion: Document the intentional split in a comment on handleSemanticFieldComparison: the handler intentionally skips relation resolution because comparison is cross-relational. Extract the shared top/min_shared/min_logdice parameter extraction into a standalone resolveBaseParams method that all three handlers call, separating it from the relation-specific resolveRelationConfig. This makes the commonality explicit and the intentional difference (no relation for comparison) visible.

root_causes: The comparison endpoint was added after the explore endpoints, inheriting the same parameter names but skipping the relation parameter; the shared preamble buildExploreRequest was not retrofitted to accommodate the no-relation case.
possible_false_positives: Boilerplate import block duplication flagged between ExplorationHandlers.java and CollocateProfileComparator.java is not design duplication — these are Java import statements
